Ive had this guy for over a year now. This has been through so many different situations and always comes up as a winner.

BATTERY:
The normal battery is perfectly fine for most, however if you want to crank it loud for long, get the bigger battery. That is what I went for.  Its a snap (literally) to install and Ive played it at FULL volume with hip hop/dubstep for 11 hours. Just insane battery life. at 60% it will last around 48 hours of continuous play. It charges relatively slow but thats because i have the 100 W/hr battery, which is expected. In sleep mode, it goes into a REALLY low power use state, and ive left on for a week, and only lost one bar of battery. halve everything ive said for the regular battery.


SOUND: 
Crank this up outside and youll hear it clearly for a quarter of a mile. They are NOT joking when they say this has serious output. Quality is good too, its similar in audio profile to my Sonos play 5 (gen2). The super deep bass is a bit lacking. However, the EQ settings will quickly fix that. Its got a neutral-warm midtone, and a clear, defined high end. Even using the EQ to crank the sub didnt get any distortion at all. This speaker loves to be played loud for a long time. its the PERFECT beach/ pool party speaker (just dont get it wet). Even though theres a ported sub, it makes no unwanted noises like port chuffing or popping. 

EASE OF USE: 
Almost as easy as it can get. paring process is painless (like it should be) connection is stable, and over long distances. the EQ settings and "menu" are easy to use. Only downside is every time you make one adjustment to the EQ, the music pauses for a very short time. thats my only nit pick. Super useful to plug your phone into the USB on the back for charging. 

The sleep mode is super useful too. Just touch one of the lights on top and bam, its awake. 

SIZE: 
Its not a small speaker, thats for sure. But if you take this to any party, youre now the life of that party. its bulky, but for good reason, all that space improves the bass response. Its also got chonky rubber feet on the bottom that really keep it from moving.

I purchased the EXOS-9 over others in this price class because it is the only one that does not use "passive radiators", and it has an EQ you can tune. All of the speakers are actually speakers. There are a total of five. One subwoofer, two mid-bass drivers and two tweeters. The subwoofer has its own separate amplifier. It's a difference you can hear - and feel. On some music, I actually needed to use the EQ to turn the bass down a little. The mid-range response is clear and the highs are crisp. Is it "Hi-Fi"? No, but remember that we're talking about a portable Bluetooth speaker that uses digital sound processing to make up for the deficiencies in the small speakers. In some program material, you can hear the DSP chip compressing and limiting levels to prevent distortion.Bluetooth is also far from high fidelity, but this unit does have Qualcomm's AptX Bluetooth chip which is capable of much higher quality sound than regular Bluetooth audio. (You'll need a phone that supports AptX audio) Most people won't even notice, but I am a more critical listener than most. For best fidelity, ditch wireless altogether and use the 3.5mm cable input.

 In my 14x35 family room, it shakes the walls with plenty of well-balanced sound. Outside, it provides ample volume. More than enough to annoy your neighbors to the point of calling the cops. When cranked up to near maximum, the battery life is about 6 hours. At reasonable listening levels, it'll go 20 hours no problem. I like the fact you can remove the battery pack and swap out a fresh one. What will you do with your JBL or Sony when the battery wears out in a few years? Their batteries are sealed inside and not user replaceable.

This is what I would call an "adult" Bluetooth speaker. There is no fancy light show and it isn't splash resistant. It looks more at home in an oak bookshelf than sitting on a tailgate. But it will happily do tailgate duty and do a good job too.

I hate to bash the Aiwa Exos-9 since so many people seem to love it like it's their baby but it did not live up my expectations or the hype. I would only consider it a value of 5-star for $100 and 4-star for $150 BECAUSE of what it is - portable and powerful. It lacks high-end sound quality which drops it out of an A-League audio purist' speaker. Simply put, it is not pleasing enough to cost $300. I would try the Harman Kardan Go Play for $180 as a cheaper and possibly superior rival. I cannot recommend anything for sure in place of the Aiwa because I have no real life experience with anything in this class but from my research I would consider the Harman Kardan go play ($180), the Fluance fi70 ($500) and possibly the DiamondBoxx ($500-$1700) as the nearest competitors.
 
For most casual listeners I think they will be pleased enough to enjoy the E9. That is not meant to be a put down but some people don't have elevated hearing like I do (my mom and one of my brothers are examples). 
However, audio enthusiast / purist will probably recognize the E9 as a ho-hum (bland, lifeless, run of the mill) speaker with some added bass for bass-heads. It is OK for a hard thumping speaker in a box but it isn't even as loud as I expected it to be. 

The music never made me forget I was listening to it out of a box because it has that box-congestion sound - like a nasally voice, this speaker has a box sound that it couldn't hide.This was the deal breaker for me. 

Build quality seemed sturdy and like care was given. The BT connectivity was fast as a snap. The unit is heavy and pretty large, thus I would call it a semi-portable speaker: You can definitely manage to take it with you but it will be a little bit of trouble doing so.

The built-in equalizer and touchscreen are awkward amenities. Each time you adjust the EQ up or down the sound cut out then went back up to the set volume. I found this irritating. 
The touchscreen isn't very user-friendly or responsive, either. In fact, I had to rub my hand across the top back and forth before it would ever recognize I was trying to use the buttons. I never could quite figure out a fail-proof method to get the touchpad to light-up and respond, I had to keep tapping and rubbing it until it would finally wake-up. Not a deal breaker for me but a nuisance like the EQ.

In conclusion, no amount of adjusting frequency bands is going to be enough for a speaker with the limitations that the Exos-9 has. Standing bass waves, the boxy sound signature, cheap Class D amplification and lifeless mid/highs held me back from keeping the E9 or considering it a deal at $250-$300. I am happy to have back the space it took up and my hard earned $$.

I stumbled upon the aiwa exos 9 online and was quickly remenisced about this familiar brand as i had various portable cd players and boom boxes from my youth. The amount of positive reviews ive seen from here, amazon and certain forums were convincing for me to try.

I was thoroughly impressed by how quickly the item shipped to me considering how i dont live in the continental u.s. The speaker has a nice size and i love the look and styling of it. It was light, but not at all flimsy or something of inferior quality. I just wish the volume knob was black as well to give it a steath look all around.

And now for the best part: sound. This thing is off the heezy! It was just what i was looking for - loud, tight bass, nice mids and highs. I also love how quickly it is able to sync with my android phone even from afar (30 yards). I even compared it side by side to the beats beatbox that my brother has and my girlfriends bose soundlink 3. I already knew it was superior, but seeing the faces of my brother and girlfriend when they heard my aiwa exos 9 compared to theirs was priceless. Especially when they heard how much i paid for it. Lets just say their sodium levels went up hehe. Awesome job aiwa, and keep up the good work best buy. Yall are killing it right now as dont even bother to shop on amazon when it comes to electronics.

First off, I'm 50 years old. I was around way back in the 80's (hehe), when Boomboxes were all the rage, and I've owned most of the top dogs. JVC's M70/90, PC-3, PC-5, and quite a few others I that I can't recall the models. Over the past 20 years I've purchased (and still own) the original KaBoom, RV-DP100/200, and more recently the original Harman Kardon G0-Play (phenomenal), and the Altec Lansing IMT-810 (Very underrated)! My first (and only) BT portable was the Kicker Amphitheater (which is the only one listed that doesn't take batteries).

Not being able to actually physically see, touch & hear the Exos-9, and only going by all of the extremely positive feedback I've been reading on various sites, I decided to take my chance. So I went all in and ordered two!

Well they arrived today and all I can say is I'm elated that I went for it. These things ROCK! I thought the Altec 810 was a beast, and it is, but the Exos 9 is heads and tails above it, and all others I've listed! The highs are crisp, but not overbearing. The mids are where they need to be, and the bass is insane! I highly doubt that any other battery operated portable can reach the volumes this monster does. And without distorting!!

Some people say it's to heavy to be considered a portable. I say fill up your water weights and get real. it's around 15 pounds, it's not that heavy. Try hauling around a JVC M-90 with 10 D-cells for a day, and then get back to me about weight.

Anyways, for anyone who's on the fence about ordering this bad boy, I say get it. or better yet, get two and pair them. You won't regret it !!

And on a side note for those who are looking for a carry case, I just picked up two American Tourister "atmosphera two" luggage carriers at Walmart (item number 62221-1041), and they are a perfect fit. You can even leave the Exos 9 in the carrier while listening to perhaps the best Boombox ever!

This speaker is amazing! The highs are as crisp as burnt toast, and the bass will blow the windows out of your house. Finally, someone has produced a home stereo-quality speaker that is portable - I love that I can bring the party anywhere! Best $300 I have ever spent.

This speaker is spectacular! The volume, base and clarity are things I would expect from a wired speaker, I've owned high-end Bose 901 speakers in the past and currently own large floor model JBL's. The Exos-9 blows them away. I find myself giving demos and raving about this speaker. I'm ordering another to link and pair.

Hands down the best bluetooth speaker for the money on the market! There is no possible way you will dislike this speaker. Operates at full power unplugged 200 watts! You can link two together with a 50-foot blue tooth range and have true stereo sound. One word: Awesome!!

The sound quality of the Exos 9 is simply the best I have heard at that price point (or even twice price) . I am surprised, and frankly relieved, they did not price the speaker at the same as the Bose Sound Touch System IV. I can't wait to hear new products from the resurrected Aiwa brand.

I was very excited about trying a larger speaker. My tiny bluetooth speakers were great for the office and taking on a business trip, but just couldn't get the job done in a large room.... Let alone outdoors. Aiwa has built the perfect speaker for what I needed. I love the interface and ease of setup but make no mistake, this thing is all about the quality of sound. It is big! It is not ideal for taking on a plane, but has a chargeable battery to move around with ease. Go ahead....embarrass your friends and remind them what music is supposed to sound like! There is nothing else like it. I'm ordering a second one so I can have paired stereo in my backyard.

I bought the 3-Year Standard Geek Squad Protection for my treadmill. I’ve used this plan before with other workout equipment, and it really has helped me in the past. Best Buy has always taken care of the repair or replacement once the plan is active, which gives me peace of mind since I use my treadmill every day.

There are a few things to keep in mind. Even though you buy a 3-year plan, it doesn’t start until the manufacturer’s warranty ends. I had a treadmill in the past with very poor support from the manufacturer, and Best Buy couldn’t step in until that warranty was over. That part was frustrating because I felt stuck for a while.

Once the Geek Squad plan kicked in, I still had some trouble getting clear answers. I spoke with many different customer service reps, and a lot of them gave me different information. It took a while before I reached someone who understood the issue and helped me move forward. When the right person steps in, the service is great, but getting there can take patience.

One more thing to note is that once you use the protection plan for a replacement, the plan is considered fulfilled. If that happens early in the coverage period, you lose the remaining time.

Even with those challenges, I still feel the plan has good value, especially for fitness equipment that takes daily wear and tear. I would recommend it, as long as you know what to expect.
Equipment may break or have electrcal gremlins.  A true benefit (value/peace of mind) for the relatively small expense.

Customer support was very nice but informed me it was gong to take six weeks for them to get the part to us. It was a cosmetic piece so we can use the bike but six weeks to replace a part someone forgot to put in the box?

PROS
--------
-The price is great
-Solid construction
-Built in speakers
-Comfortable seat
-The Explore the World app synch easily and gives you six free courses with beautiful scenery
- Very Quiet
- Smooth operation

CONS
---------
-USB port charges a phone but not enough amps to charge an iPad
-The bike has bluetooth out but not in so the built in speakers are not bluetooth capable
-The USB charging port does not support audio so you need to use two cords if you want to play audio and charge at the same time - minor but why?
-The seat is adjustable but slides forward very forcefully when unlocked. It takes a good amount of force to push it back up and lock it in place. Smaller people might have a little trouble with that. It really could use a spring or a piston to make adjustments easier and smoother.
- Unable to synch with apps like Zwift or Rouvy but might be able to if a cadence sensor is purchased separately and attached to the pedals
- The Explore the World app is nice but has very limited on screen info. Just distance and time but not cadence or resistance and the lower LCD screen that is still visible when an iPad is in the holder can not display cadence and resistance at the same time. They should fix one or the other.
- The seat back is hard plastic with a strong lumbar curve. This can be uncomfortable if its not a good fit for your shape and size. It would be great if they offered a mesh back as an option.

Overall its a great bike .The Cons I listed are mostly minor things and most of them are things they could fix pretty easily and inexpensively if they wanted to. The sliding seat is something that would require a bit of a redesign but they should look into that with the next upgrade because if you have multiple people using the bike and some are less muscular this could be annoying if not slightly hazardous.

This ProForm Carbon T7 Treadmill is exactly what I needed in our home gym to allow for an intense workout when I need it and to also allow for uphill walking while I read or catch up on my favorite shows. The iFit platform has been a hit in our house as well, the coached runs add a dynamic I wouldn’t have had otherwise, and we can track our fitness activity using our accounts on the treadmill and on our ProForm bike. For more intense workouts on the treadmill, I find the cushioning to be more forgiving than running on the road, and more comfortable than other treadmills I’ve owned in the past. As a Physical Therapist, I am always aware of the level of impact throughout a week of exercise, and I feel that I can comfortably log more miles when using the Carbon T7. The incline feature has been nice, this allows me to dial back speed and still get a decent workout. My husband has been walking at an incline while watching lectures or when in long video conference meetings. And as I mentioned, the iFit has been a nice added bonus that I did not expect to really utilize, for both the added instruction and engagement, and also for the cumulative stats that we can track between our two ProForm pieces of equipment. Assembly was straightforward, as instructed the assembly does require two people and can be completed in approx. one hour and it's fairly heavy so two people is a must. The treadmill folds up if you need to clear up space, it has a nice locking mechanism so you can easily fold it up and move it around. Thus far the product has been great. I was given this product in exchange for my honest and unbiased opinion, and I will certainly recommend this to my patients who are looking for a home treadmill based on the simplicity of operation, incline function, speed variability, comfort, and for the iFit that is included.

Wow...wow...wow, let me start by saying I am a bit of an audio snob. I crave loud, crisp music with clear highs and deep lows. I needed a portable speaker to listen to at work in a shop type atmosphere so I needed something that could crank, but also something small, with a battery that I could take on the go for board game nights, or the impromptu music party. One of my best friends owns the Harmon Kardon Onyx and I was always impressed with sound, but it was to big for my taste. I first purchased the Sony h.ear.go hi res speaker based on the reviews. It was tiny, packed with features, and offered a decent battery life expectancy. In the real world, it sounded nice at very low volumes, but anything over 75 percent it would distort and 100 percent was maybe twice as loud as my iPad pro but with horribly distorted muddy bass. I watched a bunch of reviews to see what would be a good replacement and settled with the JBL Extreme. It was about quadruple the size of the sony, but was so loud and clear and had pretty awesome bass. The major annoyance though was bluetooth drop out. It was terrible. With almost every track change the music would drop out for a second and a few times drop out so long it would disconnect from my iPad all together. I returned it for another one, but made the snap decision on the Braven 2200m even though there wasn't one on display and very little on the Internet about it. But the box seemed promising, and figured a 300 dollar speaker has to at least sound good. The very first song I played was Dire Straight money for nothing. I played at 100 percent volume which absolutely blew me away! I tried several music genres, Metallica's Nothing Else Matters, T Pain's Up Down, Ella Fitzgerald and Louis Arstrongs Dream a little Dream and several others. No matter what I threw at, I could not get it to distort. Very impressive. It is every bit twice as loud as the jbl extreme, has amazing stereo separation and bass you could literally feel in your body. If you want a good example of how it sounds but your local store doesn't have one on display, go to home theater and check out the Sonos 3 and Sonos 5, I would put it somewhere between the 2 of them. Better bass response than the 3, not quite as much as the 5 but definitely the clarity of the 5. This thing cranks so loud, if you'd didn't see it right away, you would think your listening to a really large higher end shelf system. Packaging was great, build quality is really impressive, but most importantly the sound is astonishing for such a small package!

SUMMARY: The Bang & Olufsen Beosound Explore (‘B&O Explore’ from this point forward) will NEVER be a bad choice. The bass can use some work. BUT, it feels great in the hand, aesthetically very cool to look at, it’s easy to set up, it sounds great to the ears, the volume goes quite high before there is any distortion, the battery power lasts remarkably long, and it is amazingly rugged. It also sounds great through the gamut of music genres (read below about bass). 

I use this with an iPad Air 2nd gen (iPadOS 14.8), as well as an iPhone 11 PRO (iOS 14.8). Setup using the app is self-explanatory and very quick. The app isn’t bad; sometimes it does become unpaired from the iDevice, and it’s frequent enough to be a minor nuisance. Luckily, it happens when you start using it, instead of during playback. I know B&O like to keep it simple, but the app is extremely boring to look at; like reading a newspaper because it’s literally black and white. The B&O Beosound Explore allows you to take your music to places, and in conditions, that you couldn’t previoiusly take a Bluetooth speaker. It’s made for adventure because it’s rugged and water-resistent and design-forward. The app needs to be exciting like the product it controls. 

I’ve taken this hiking a few times, once where I was out the entire day in the middle of nowhere, playing music at 50%+ volume, and it lasted the entire 8-ish hours with battery power to spare. I would not have expected that at all, since the volume was at 50% or a bit more. The B&O Explore battery charges quickly via USB-Type C. It was drizzling rain and chilly, and it sat there on a picnic bench while I grilled out with my kids. It feels very well-made. The size is awesome, because it’s easy to pack. The carabiner that comes with, and the nylon loop, is great to hang it on a backpack or a thicker tree branch. The bottom is somewhat rubberized, because it did not slide off of smooth surfaces. It is shipped with a silicone ‘cap’ for the top (controls) and the bottom (Type-C plug). 

I’ve tried most genres of music with the B&O Explore; it performs extremely well. The bass only becomes an issue if you are listening to rock or rap music that is bass-intensive. BASS-INTENSIVE; in general, it handles bass very well. At that point, you understand the limitation of the unit, as you hear the bass ‘bottom out’; that is, it gets to a point where the unit can no longer reproduce the amount / frequency of bass, and begins to distort. On a very positive note about the bass ‘bottoming out’, instead of becoming an utterly distorted mess, the bass very discernably and cleanly just cuts off. I say this is a positive thing because if I’m listening to a song and the speaker can’t handle the bass, it is very desirable to continue listening to clear-sounding music, versus the bass distortion. I’ve never heard that from any speaker in my life, and I do wonder if that ‘feature’ was intentional by Bang & Olufsen...if it was, I thank you for that. There are competitors that make a BT speaker of similar size that handles the bass a bit better, but I can’t take them hiking in the rain; I would no doubt opt for the latter.

This speaker is what you’d expect for something this size. I enjoy collecting and using speakers of all sizes and am an outdoorsy type person that believe music should be available in all situations. The size is small and portable and has been good for battery life so far, its supposed to get 27 hours which I have yet to push it that far. I also like the USB C charger option as its one less obstacle I have to worry about when charging, unlike my other speakers which are micro-usb. 

It is beautifully crafted with a premium feel. However, for being shock proof and claiming to take a beating it seems too nice of a product. Most rugged and water resistant products are lined with some sort of protection that typically can absorb impacts, but the nice aluminum finish looks like it’ll get damaged easily even though it claims to be scratch resistant. That concern aside, I think its great that a premium speaker can handle the downsides of a portable lifestyle! Adding to the premium look and feel, it has a nice weight to it. I associate a heavy weight (not too heavy) product with quality, cheap lightweight-speakers usually don’t have that premium or long-lasting sound. 

The next thing I judge a speaker by is sound level, aka how powerful it is. Even for a smaller speaker, I expect a good level of sound that can clearly protrude general noises and conversations without distortion. I would rate this speaker a 3.5/5 for power level. I would test it out in my garage while working in a partially enclosed area and max volume wasn’t too loud which I find slightly disappointing. I would even go down the driveway and it was moderately acceptable. Can’t expect too much from a personal size speaker, but its one of the criteria I still measure a speaker by.

Overall, it’s one of the more premium looking and feeling speakers I’ve ever owned, and having 360 degree sound is a great design feature. Pairing was super quick and easy, it came with a USB cable but not the charging block to plug into, but that seems to be the norm these days. I would rate the speaker the 4.5/5 stars and would be a great gift for anyone.

The Bang & Olufsen Beosound Explore durable portable wireless Bluetooth speaker is very impressive. It is amazing how simple it was to set up. The Beosound also has amazing sound for such a small package. Not to mention it is made of metal and very solid. Being made of anodized aluminum, it can withstand a fall of about 4.5 feet. Add the IP67 rating which means it can be submerged in 3 feet of water for 30 minutes.

This speaker is packaged very well in a small package. See the picture for what is in the box. There is the speaker, a USB-C to USB-A cable, carabiner, and a quick start guide. Also there is a picture of the speaker in relation to a pop can for reference. It is very small. The speaker came with a 70% charge out of the box. The Beosound is rated for 27 hours on a full charge. I fully charged the speaker. I have used it for about 3 hours so far and there is still 87% charge left.

It was easy to set up the speaker. My Android phone recognized the Beosound right away and prompted me to download the app. You download the app and complete the set up. It was really cool how Bang & Olufsen managed the EQ settings. I have seen manufacturers manage this in many different ways. This was the simplest approach I have ever seen. There are preset listening modes (see picture). Click on the circle and you see Relaxed, Warm, Energetic and Bright (see picture). You use your finger to slide and select the setting you want. Do this while your audio is playing so you can hear the change is sound to make it perfect to you. Right = high treble and high bass. Left is low treble and low bass. This is pure brilliance. And the sound is FANTASTIC. I cannot believe how clear and loud this speaker is. Are you going to get mind thumping bass, of course not. Look at how small it is. Will you get good and clear sound across the spectrum, most definitely.

I rate this 5 out of 5 stars. This speaker is small and very durable. The included carabiner and attached strap make it easy to transport. You will not be disappointed.

Love Bang & Olufsen and if you know the name too, you will know they make some excellent speakers. The Beosound Explore is a great little speaker a little bigger than a size of a soda can that packs some great sounds. App is plain but still full of features to suit your needs.

In the box is one superbly built speaker. Nice and heavy (not too heavy) and super portable. You can even pair it with another Explore to enable stereo mode. Easily can take it on the go with you anywhere. It does come with a charging USB C TO A cable, but it warned, you need your own adapter for it.

App is nice once you DL and pair the speaker to your device (I used my phone). Can change the volume, presets, check battery life and can customize your own equalizer settings. I actually like the circle-type equalizer and I can change it while listening to music and hear real time changes to make sure I get it to where I want it. 

What's great about this speaker is the sound quality for mids and highs. The 360 sound is amazing and can fill up any room and can get loud. Now with a little flaw, the bass ranges from non-existent to pretty meh. When turning up the speaker I'd say past 60%, the bass gets distorted and starts to go out. No oomph that I was hoping for. For non-bass heavy music, it sounds amazing though. Mid level volume sound great and even using the equalizer can sorta save the bass a little.

Battery life is where it shines as well. I charged the B&O to 100% and streamed to it roughly 6-7 hrs a day for 4 days and it's at 58% at the moment. I am also not blasting it at max volume though but also using my own personal presets.

It's a great BT speaker but the bass falls short in my opinion of B&O super high standards that I am used to.

The Bang & Olufsen - Durable Portable Wireless Bluetooth Speaker in an Anthracite-colored, hard-coat anodized aluminum casing is rugged, beautifully manufactured and produces excellent 360 degree quality sound. 

Why take away a star for such a stellar outdoor-ready, Bang & Olufsen high-quality sound speaker? Because the controlling B&O application is not ready for prime time.

During set-up, I downloaded the B&O app from the Playstore and proceeded to chose the Beosound Explore from the list, as instructed. After myriad attempts, fully charging the speaker, turning the speaker & Bluetooth off and on, restarting and reloading the app, the app still could not find my new Beosound. It hasn't yet. 

This is even though my Samsung phone found it immediately and connected immediately. And even though my Samsung television found it immediately and connected beautifully. And my Asus computer did the same.

This small, portable, light-weight, well-built speaker that comes with a carabiner and built in strap to carry on your belt, vest or backpack sounds great with all these sources and is really impressive with its full-bodied sound. 

I am just sorry that I can't get the app to recognize that it exists, so that I can remotely control it. 

B&O, it is time for a bug fix.

I wanted to be both awed and impressed by the Bang & Olufsen Beosound Explore Portable Speaker. My expectations were based solely on the stellar reputation of B & O. While unboxing I was thrilled with it’s solid build, compact size (slightly larger than a can of soda), and high-end, scratch-resistant finish. Advertised as water resistant for up to 30 minutes in 1 meter of water and boasting the ability to withstand a fall of up to 1-½ meters this speaker begged to be taken adventuring on the weekend. Unfortunately, once I connected my phone via Bluetooth I quickly realized that while the 360-degree sound it offered easily filled small spaces this speaker was not the powerhouse that I was expecting it to be. Even after using the app to adjust the sound there was still some distortion at high volumes and the base was lacking. While I really liked the aesthetics and features of this speaker it just didn’t deliver the commanding performance that I was expecting from the sticker price and brand name.

A lot of people say you have to try the B&O app for it to perform better. True. The app does help, but not much..... take my advice, don’t buy this speaker. I recently bought the Bose Flex speaker which just came out for $149, that one is FAR better than this “Speaker”.

There are good things about this computer, but there are many more things that are bad.

The good:
It is aesthetically very pleasing. The RGB components seem to have good quality LEDs and the cables seem to be managed well (in the visible areas). The case is also pleasant to look at.
It is also packaged very well. It shipped in the case box and packed with soft foam. It also has the hardening foam inside the case to keep the gfx card from being too stressed during shipping.
Choice of components seem to be good (on the surface) when it comes to expanability.

The bad:
Out of the box this PC is not a great experience to use. Once you get loaded into Windows 11, you're immediately met with popups from the pre-installed gigabyte software for the motherboard. Pre-installed software isn't the issue, it's the fact that it spams you until you interact. A mistake I made was interacting with these. One piece of software called control center offered the ability to update your drivers with one click. Nice! Well, this led to instability and blue screens post updates. Not a problem, I'll just re-install windows from the local recovery partition. Well now my USB ports frequently disconnects. Sometimes they'll reconnect after a few minutes, other times I have to hard reset my computer to get functionality back. And if you don't hard reset, the USB ports still won't work even in bios which means it's an issue with the USB controller on the mobo or the chipset. Alright let me flash my bios with the most current stable release. Still issues. Let me install a 30 dollar pcie USB card. That fixed it. The USB controller on the MOBO is just easily overwhelmed on a computer that's work more than some cards. Great. Next I need to install some software that's only available from the windows store. No windows store. This might be a plus for some people, but not for me. Some software like iCloud and EarTrumpet are only available on windows store. Okay now let me try to re-install the windows store. Powershell script you'd normally use doesn't work. Built in store troubleshooter doesn't work. Restarting the windows service for installing the store, doesn't work. The only way I found to install the store was to go into the Xbox app, goto settings and they said required services for this app to were missing. From there I could install it when admin powershell wouldn't. for some reason. So now I have the windows store. cool. So let's review what I had to do to make this computer stable and able to be usable for daily uses for myself.
Uninstall gigabyte software that would auto update to unstable drivers
Re-install windows twice to repair instability
Flash the BIOS to repair instability
update drivers to be on the proper stable channels
Install a $30 dollar USB 3.0 card
Install the Xbox app to install the windows store, then uninstall the xbox app

The Audio Pro Addon T3 is an amazing Bluetooth speaker. For its size it puts out beautiful sound. Such a delight to the ears. The bass isn't overdone but rather tight and punchy. Crisp clean high end as well. Overall excellent sound. I recommend this speaker based on sound alone.

This is an elegant, well built cabinet that contributes to the amazing output of this device.

I wouldn't call this thing a "Bass blaster" but that's not the intent here - Its a "reference sound" that you get - Rich, full mids and lows, with precise and crisp highs that (to me) deliver also. The bass is rich and detailed, but is not so deep that it gets muddy. The midrange is good, but most speakers this size get a little mud in the middle, and this one is better than most.

The power cord was surprisingly a standard 2-prong cord which means replaceability isn't a problem should it be damaged, and the power supply is fully integrated into the main box. The other nice thing for global travelers such as I am, is that the box includes a standard US and an EU AC plug.

Overall this thing is QUALITY QUALITY QUALITY! Sound, packaging, industrial design, and battery life are all excellent! Its an unusual form factor for portable Bluetooth audio, but if its your style, the output won't disappoint!

Pros:

Loud

Functionality

This speaker is probably the best designed speaker that is also backed by really good sound. I have the grey version which is contrasted in the front by three black speaker grills. That top has a very nice copper finish with quality, strong buttons. It also has a carrying handle matching the color of the speaker. The speaker sits of 4 feet supports that give it an upward angle, a very nice touch. All ports are accessed on the back. This includes the AUX, USB, and Power port. High quality look and feel. The battery life was very good, listening to 100% volume for at least 5hours. I was not able to push it to the end, but was impressed so far. I was very happy with the loudness of the speaker, but it is definitely limited to a smaller environment if there is a lot of background noise. The sound was balanced, with the bass complementing the highs and lows well. The bass had a balance of depth and punch without being mottled. Vocals came through with clarity. I was pleased this speaker did not distort or washout the highest highs. It's clear Audio Pro went to great lengths to produce a quality product that implemented high-end design. Works great with Bluetooth and AUX. Setup takes no time at all. Multiple function buttons for volume, track change, etc. Bluetooth range was at least 30feet with through walls. Quick Charging.

Cons:
Slight inconsistency in sound
Power cord non-proprietary

As good as the sound is, the were some inconsistencies in volume change. When playing certain tracks with louder and quieter vocals, the loudness would be much less than the set point when the vocals dropped to lower levels. I haven't been able to determine whether this was on the source side or the speaker side and should not be a major concern. The power cable uses the type you'd see on a Blu-Ray player or HDTV. I would have liked to see something more common/standard. However, this is very likely due to the quality of the battery and speaker requiring and larger power output. Overall, I highly recommend this speaker.

When I received this product I was blown away. It looks absolutely amazing. Then when I connected it to my phone it was even better. The sound was clear and had great bass. Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker - White
The Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker is a compact little package that has a very unique look. When you first see it you might be surprised how small it is. I measured it at 5” high, 5 ¼” deep and 8 ½” long. Then when I picked it up I was surprised how heavy it felt. This is one solid box. The back is mostly metal and includes the AC connection, a USB port that is for charging only, AUX 3.5mm in and I counted 16 screws. The body seems to be made out of a very solid plastic and when you rap on it, it feels very solid. The controls on the top are done in a very nice looking metal plate and button controls follow that same theme. It looks very good and also at the top is the leather handle. On the front is three speakers behind metal grills. Two 2 ¾” dome tweeters and in the middle one 3.5” woofer. It supports Bluetooth 4.0 which is not the latest standard and that is too bad. With such great build quality you almost expected better. Still this is the most common Bluetooth standard you will find in most devices today. This means easy parting and good sound. One of the standout feature here is the rated battery life. Up to 30 hours at 50% volume and 12 hours at 100% volume. I can’t see it ever being used at !00% but who knows. This is a pure digital Class D amplifier. Class D amplifier are very power friendly, respond fast and are very common. This amplifier is rated at 5 watts to the twiters and 15 watts to the woofer. And the sound, well it is good and shines more on the top end. The bass shows up but it is not overwhelming. I played it in an open space and that helped it breth. It can put out room filling sound if needed. In a more closed environment like a moving vehicle the bass didn’t seem to have the same impact. I had 8 hours of playback without any problem so I wouldn’t be surprised that it can keep going. The market is loaded with portable Bluetooth speakers. This one stands out as being very well built. It doesn’t have the flashing LEDs some of the portable speakers have. But it is very unique with excellent build quality. One thing that seems missing here is lack of waterproofing. As well built and being this portable that seem like a bit of an oversight. Maybe that’s the trade off for this sound quality. The Audio Pro Addon T3 has a lot of competition in its price range. But if you're looking for day long battery life in the most portable package that is sure get smiles the T3 deserves a good look.

The Audio Pro Addon T3 is a smallish portable speaker that has pure, clean, neutral sound and a retro look. The sound stays sharp at any volume level. The Addon T3 is good for small to medium listening spaces.Perfect for work desk, dorm room or at home. It has a killer battery that'll last 30 hours when playing at half volume or 12 hours at full volume. The rechargeable battery is removable in case you ever need to replace it.

This is an attractive, great sounding speaker that provides form and function.

I have tried many BT portable speakers including high end ones from Bose. I was amazed by the sound quality from Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker. Even at the max sound, I couldn't notice any distortion but high quality perfect sound for every kind of music I payed. 

Outstanding battery life. Quick Bluetooth connection. Additional aux input. Classy look with amazing audio quality. What else you want from a tiny portable bluetooth speaker? 

Downside? yes a couple of them: You cannot control T3's volume from your bluetooth connected device. Can't use USB port to power up devices such as Chromecast.

Overall, I am very satisfied with T3 and highly recommend it for audio enthusiasts.

This speaker blew me away. It is probably one of best looking speakers I've seen. It looks great on your bookshelf. It looks elegant and refined, unlike most speakers that look "sporty." But, that elegance (at least in my opinion) means I won't be taking this out anywhere rugged. Maybe on a picnic, and while this sounds fantastic, I think it's main selling point is its design.

Sound...

Is fantastic. It has a rich satisfying sound, which brought back memories of the original Bose Wave Radio. It is amazing that this small of a box can blast such deep satisfying and resounding bass, while maintaining crystal clear clarity. The sound truly is something to behold. And it is plenty loud with no distortion. I played classical music, Disney movie music, podcasts and oldies and they all sounded great. The quality is truly remarkable. 

A few good things...

Obviously the sound and the looks, but one of the first things I've ever seen is a replaceable battery. Obviously this is new and I've had no reason to change the battery but it gives me comfort. It has a USB charging port for your phone, and since this has a rated battery of about 40 hours, it's going to outlast your phone and it's nice to be able to charge your phone. It also has an Aux cable, which in my opinion is a must. Bluetooth is great, and convenient, but I've always run into situations where the Bluetooth connection just wouldn't work. The craftsmanship. This baby has some heft to it, which I appreciate it. It is durable and you can just tell the persons who designed, engineered, and manufactured this speaker love what they do and paid close attention to the details. 

Some little quirks...

No screen. This is personal preference and not a huge deal, but there's no screen or more specifically, no volume meter. The buttons are also clicky; and loud. This sounds like a dumb thing to say, but they just have a loud unsatisfying click. The buttons don't feel flimsy, they just sound it.

All in all...

If you are looking for an elegant, small form factor, Bluetooth speaker with great sound, this is the one. If sound matters to you and is at the top of your must have list, then I don't think you can do much better than this speaker.

I was a bit taken aback when I saw the box of the product when it arrived. It was smaller than I was expecting. It had a good sturdy weight and is a great looking device. I did as instructed and charged it fully before use. At the time of writing this, I played it 4-5 hours and didn't have to charge again. It will also charge your devices as well! Now about the most important part of all: the sound. 

The sound quality is beyond amazing! It's size is deceiving in the sound that the little thing puts out! It's approx 8x11x8 and 5 lbs. The bass is great! It is extremely loud without distortion though I was afraid to turn it up all the way! I'm very pleased to have this awesome speaker and it seems now I have to fight my son for it! LOL! He's loving it as much as I am!

The pure sound, extremely rugged build and ease of use provide a super portable speaker. This speaker has all the required controls in a simple array. I paired it easily with my phone and also my desktop, no re-pairing or second tries, it just pairs right immediately - that's a really nice reflection of the electronics. You also have an auxiliary input so you can attach directly to your device if desired. I actually had a small fender modelling amp which can run through a PC and you can use Fender Fuse software to add effects - I say this because I wanted to see how the effects would sound by playing a guitar through the Fender amp and then feeding the AUX output on the amp to the Audio Pro speaker. The amp is only 5 watts but now I have a 25 RMS watt system and both can function off batteries nice. By the way RMS means 'root mean square' so when a manufacturer uses this it's important because many manufacturers will use peak watts and it sounds like more. So this is a good manufacturer because they could have listed 35 peak watts if they chose to. One problem with guitar amps is that you can have a little noise sometimes and this test showed that the sound through the Audio Pro would remain pure over quite a lot of string banging. So I do recommend this little marvel highly, it's not cheap but it is worth it for the pure sound and portability.

This is not your average Bluetooth speaker. The first thing you will notice is the 2 wonderful textures. The front has a rough texture, almost like a rhino lining of a pickup bed. It feels very rough, tough and durable. The back of the unit is like a brushed aluminum. It gives it almost a commercial feel. The buttons are a nice aluminum as well that have a great feel for them.

The connection of a phone to the speaker is standard like most other Bluetooth speakers. You turn it on then hit the pair button. You then find it on the list of available devices on your phone and select it to pair. There is a button that will tell you how much battery life remaining.

The speaker sounds amazing! I played every type of music I could possible find on Pandora and my phone's media and it all sounded so good. I did not play it on full blast because i like to "break in" my speakers first before I push them too hard. The clarity of the music was very rich and vibrant while not being overpowered by messy bass. The balance is very nice and crisp.

The battery life is another one of the strong suites. It has such a long battery life that it even allows you to use the device as a battery charger for your phone. I have been using this regularly for the past few days and the battery status level is still showing full.

I really love this speaker and will be taking it with me everywhere I go in case there happens to be a need for some sweet music. It will be clutch.

I cannot say enough good things about the Audio Pro Addon T3.
It is Honestly the best bluetooth speaker I have used. The sound quality is excellent from low volumes to even when you max it out. I never heard any distortion or strain from the speaker.

The Battery Life is excellent I took it with me to work the other day and had it playing for 9 hours straight at about half volume, and it still had more than half the charge.

The design is also excellent while it is a bit blocky it is very sturdy and well designed. 

If you are in the market for a bluetooth speaker you cannot go wrong with this one.

BT speakers are a dime a dozen and range in price from maybe $10 on up to the hundreds of dollars. This one falls in the mid-range pricewise. Build quality is very impressive - it's heavy and made out of some kind of metal that gives it heft. It runs off battery and re-charges using a standard AC plug (instead of a micro-USB cable). I think this would allow it to charge much faster, so that's a definite plus. It pairs easily with your device and even has an AUX input if you want a direct wired connection. The back contains a USB port that you can use to simultaneously charge your device from the speaker's built in battery.

The only part I don't understand is that volume changes performed on the speaker are not mirrored on your device and vice versa. That means that if you change the volume on the speaker, it doesn't show the volume correspondingly change on my device (in my case, an iPhone). So, in effect, there are two volumes - that of the speaker and that of your device. If someone can comment on why this is so (and is different from all other BT devices I've used), that would be greatly appreciated.

Love this thing. It has great sound, which is the thing most important in a speaker. It's crystal clear and has some decent bass sound. It fills our family room and kitchen without the needs for another speaker. Pairing was immediate and I was playing music 10 seconds after turning it on.

Battery life is good and it gets very loud. It's well designed and built It's not light. Definitely a carry and place somewhere speaker.

I would highly recommend this speaker.

I wasn’t sure what to think when I received this. I’m used to my Bose speaker that I’ve had for years so I compare everything to that. This isn’t quite Bose quality but they work very well. Decent sound, adequate battery life. Definitely worth considering if you’re looking for a nice portable speaker.

The Audio Pro T3 is one solid speaker. And I mean solid in both ways. Solid build with heft to let you know the speaker has some weight to it, and solid as in a great overall device. When you pick this thing up you can just tell its well built. The initial charge time is about an hour or so. And its rated at 30 hours or 12 hours of full volume. Although if you just remember to connect it when your done I doubt you'll ever run out. 

 Connectivity is as simple as it gets. Just press the pair button and find the T3 the the Bluetooth settings of whatever device you are using. One click and your ready to go. And if you have a non Bluetooth device, or just want to connect via 3.5mm there is a port for that as well. Once connected you now get to hear just how powerful a small quality speaker can be. The bass is deep and the overall sound is crisp and well just sounds like it should if that makes sense. Not over done, just right. 

 It has a nice handle for carrying it around. Its portability is a great feature that other larger speakers just cant pull off. The look is kinda old school which is very cool. It also comes in several different colors so you can match it to your other devices or furniture. 

 Overall if you are looking for a Bluetooth speaker where you arent sacrificing sound quality due to a smaller device the T3 is the way to go. Amazing solid build, well balanced sound thats just right, with simple connectivity options make for a device that is easily recommendable. You cant go wrong here.

Ill be the first to say this is NOTHING like any of my other Bluetooth speakers. It is however another Bluetooth speaker... in a much better way.

Whats in the box?
The packaging is relatively nice, nothing overly special. Comes with an EU wall adapter as well as an american wall power cord. The speaker is wrapped in a nice cloth material which makes it feel a little more special. Once you take the speaker outside all the packaging and set it on a table is when you can actually admire the beauty. 

Whats the design like?
The design of this speaker is VERY modern with a touch of retro somewhere... The handle is made out of a leather or fake leather material which at the very least feels great. The back and top around the buttons is metal which is cool to the touch and make sit feel very premium. The body has a nice texture which adds to the look of not being smooth and shiny like a cheaper speaker might. Overall I love the look!

How does it sound?
I will say the other Bluetooth speaker I use regularly is the regular Bose revolve. I LOVED nearly everything about that speaker minus the upright design. The 360 sound that thing produces is amazing though. The sound of the Audio Pro - Addon T3 though blows that thing out of the water! The crisp vocals and tight punchy bass allow for a much better listening experience. The volume the T3 can reach also is much higher than the Bose. Probably a good 25% higher and the distortion is lower than the Bose. I will say the Bose does reach a certain frequency around 75% volume that does sound deeper than the T3 surprisingly. I would much rather listen to the T3 though! Just a great speaker for the money! 

Conclusion?
The Audio Pro - Addon T3 is a speaker with quality sound in mind. There is almost nothing that let me down with the speaker other than the design is almost too good and delicate to be "portable" on your average occasion. I would highly recommend this speaker for anyone in the market! You will not be disappointed.

I was thoroughly impressed with how big the sounbd is from this speaker. Boasting a battery life of 12 hours on full volume or 30 hours at 50% volume or lower, I found that 50% volume was more than adequate for most situations. I have a ton of Bluetooth speakers, but this one blows them away. In addition to Bluetooth, it also has an auxiliary 3.5mm stereo input so you can connect the T3 to any device that has a headphone jack, even if it doesn't support Bluetooth.

On top of everything, the Addon T3 has a feature I've been wanting in Bluetooth speakers for a long time: the ability to charge your phone from the speaker. The T3 has a full-size USB output that allows you to use the speaker's battery to charge other devices in a pinch. This can be a huge lifesaver if you bring your speaker along camping or at the beach.

Simple Review: The sound quality alone earns a great recommendation from me but tie that in with a premium build that makes this speaker a easy choice.

In-depth Review: Starting with the in boxing you get what you expect. Speaker with a power cable instructions but it also includes overseas power cable too. Even when it comes to the speaker it has a quality cloth feeling wrapper protecting your new investment which is appreciated.

Unit came dead but that's no surprise but I was surprised that it took about 30 minutes for a full charge which this leads us into one of the best things about this speaker. I got over 30 hours of playing out to this! For something that is not too heavy to push that much time is amazing! One thing I think you should be aware is there no voice prompts when powering on our connecting to it etc. I also didn't enjoy the cheap feeling buttons they put on this premium build, it just ruins the experience a bit. Sounds quality is strong and deep. Voices very clear while bass strong enough for my tastes. I was surprised by the quality especially since I've never seen this brand myself but I'd say it's worth the investment. 

Add it all up and you get a great speaker for those looking for something that's not just cheap plastic. I hope my review helped you in your decision. Thanks

good:
-sound quality - wow, this little guy sounds great. nothing missing from the spectrum, from top to bottom. lively, punchy, energetic, and clean, and no shortage of volume. very impressive at this price point.

-battery life - also impressive, about 30 hours at half volume, or about half that time at full volume.

-ease of use - buttons for power, pairing, and source (BT or aux). pairing happened quickly and only took a few seconds before I was up and running.

-design - somewhat industrial design, but very high quality feel and appearance. also has a leather handle for carrying, a usb port for charging external device, and an aux to connect an external player should you need to.



bad:
-not bad by any means, but some may not like the more industrial “old school” design. i personally quite like it, but i’ve always been a function over form kind of guy myself.
-no included remote. I personally don't mind as I wouldn't use it anyways.


overall:
-highly recommended. fantastic quality of build and design at this price point.

Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker - Gray
On all their packaging, it states that this portable Bluetooth speaker is the "Sound of Scandinavia." Not only is the sound of Scandinavia, but also has the looks of Scandinavia; very clean industrial-like lines, matte gray finish with copper-colored control buttons-an extremely good looking speaker. The speaker comes with 2 cords for charging, one connects to an electrical outlet and the other is a USB that allows charging to either a computer, an ancillary power pack, etc. This speaker had no problems hooking up by Bluetooth to my Samsung telephone or my HP Spectre. It has a great range of sound with a very nice, strong box and brown leather handle. It is actually the color grey like advertised in pictures on the website. 

The only issue that I had was that the instructions were in many foreign languages, but the button identifiers did have an English option, but the instructions themselves were not in English. They must sell this unit all over the world and do not have language specific instructions for the country that this is specifically sold in. Although it is fairly simple to figure out how to run the machine, if you have worked wireless equipment before, it would not have been as simple for those people who have less experience with syncing their audio-playing devices to this Bluetooth speaker.

My critique of the Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker - White is one of excellence. The first noticeable trait was the weight, when taking it out of the box, I could immediately notice how heavy and compact it is. It is constructed of steel or some type of metal. It is well built and the way it is constructed will allow it to last for decades. I had to charge it for about ninety minutes to get a full charge. Pairing with my devices was easy as just waiting to see the Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker show up on the screen after pressing search and then tap on it to pair. The sound is rich and strong, produced by three speakers. The woofer in the middle supplies deep bass frequencies, while the two tweeters give the higher octaves necessary to make music sound complete. The Audio Pro - Addon T3 Portable Bluetooth Speaker is a sturdy and functional system worth having.

I have owned several bluetooth speakers over the years and this one ranks right up there with the best of them.

Loves:
- This unit is beautifully designed. It looks terrific on a shelf or bookcase. The leather carrying strap is a nice touch. And the metallic button layout is very sleek. The feet on the bottom give the speaker a slight upward angle which I liked.
- Battery life is outstanding at up to 30 hours. If you aren't taking it on the go, it can be left plugged into a wall outlet with the included cord. It comes with a power cord for European outlets if you plan to travel with it.
- It's loud and the bass is strong.
- Sound quality ranks up there with other well known brands.
- It has an AUX port if you need to connect a non-bluetooth device.
- You can charge your phone or other electronic device from the included USB port.

What's not to love?
- Nothing. I was thoroughly impressed with this speaker.

At this price point, it's a good buy and I recommend it for anyone looking for big sound on the go or for a stylish audio option in the home.

I'll tell you that I'm a music nut, and I love this speaker. 

Now, let me say that I've listened to, own, or have owned some very nice and not so nice bluetooth speakers. I love music and often try out speakers hoping for something better than what I have. Let me clarify that I don't buy $700-$1100 speakers, I'm talking about the $150-$300 speakers. I never found anything better than what I had, until this! 

Let me start by saying this is a very stylish speaker. Generally people notice how it looks first, then notice the sound second. That is part of the problem having such a good looking speaker. I am not that way, I look at sound first, and looks after that, but since we are on the topic, let me say that this one is a bit darker gray than in the picture - and has a beautiful gold colored control panel and a really cool brown leather handle with gold fasteners. I'm guessing this is one of the most sought after speaker color combos. 

This speaker has a real electrical cord that plugs into the back of the speaker, and it comes with not one, but two. It has a place for you to charge your phone using your phone's charging cable - a very nice touch to be able to charge while listening. And it has the much appreciated aux in jack, so you can plug in a 3.5mm standard mini plug from your satellite radio, ipod, etc. 

Turning this on and pairing was something you won't need to read about. Press and hold the power button, press and hold the pair button until the bluetooth light flashes, connect, and you are good to go. It really is this simple. 

This unit has a very long battery life though I haven't had enough time to test it - it is rated at like 30 hours or something. But if it gets 8 hours I'm good. So far, its holding up well. And you'll see there is a door on the bottom of the speaker, with special screws, so you can likely change the battery when that day sometime comes. 

The build quality is nothing less than suitable for royalty. I'm not kidding. It is amazing, amazing, amazing. Solid. And it feels heavy, but in a good way. I felt like I was carrying a $100,000 violin or something, as it is balanced, elegant, precious yet sturdy, etc. Top shelf. 

Where this speaker shines, above and beyond anything I've ever listened to, is the sound quality. There are two visible tweeters and a visible woofer. You'll feel the air pressure coming out of the hole in the back of the speaker, which is exhausting of the bass. 

The highs are perfect, and the bass is something I've never heard from any speaker in this class. But the more amazing part is that no matter what I hook up to it, it makes it sound better than it does on other speakers. No kidding. Satellite, Ipod, streaming, etc. all sound equally amazing with this speaker, something I've never experienced before with any speaker. 

I'm very critical of speakers, because I love my music. It might be Frank Sinatra, Beethoven, Aerosmith, Blake Shelton, or even some show tunes. All of these sound amazing on this speaker. I thought I knew what was a decent sound before, but I had no idea how great my music could sound on this truly amazing speaker. 

It isn't as small as you might think, in particular as bluetooth speakers have tried to get smaller and better sounding - our baseline of what size these should be has shrunk. But make no mistake, this is worth every bit of space it takes up and you will not regret buying this speaker. Frankly it is as much a decoration as an amazing speaker! The long battery life and USB charging port are only a bonus. 

Funny, I would have thought this speaker was a $700 speaker, based solely on sound. And I did blind sound tests. This was by far the best of the many speakers I own; I did this because I could not believe something so affordable could sound so amazing. It is true, it is really amazing. 

Thanks for developing a speaker that looks and sounds great but is also affordable to those who really want the best sounding speaker available. 
I only wish I could include more than 2 pics with this review, because seeing it is believing, but hearing it will be the main selling point to you. 

Oh yes, the packaging is also top shelf, a box that is worth holding onto because this is something you'll want to pass down to someone in your family some day. It takes something really special for me to feel this way, and I'm very critical, but this has sold me.
I can't believe how loud this thing is, and truly how amazing the sound is. I'm blown away. This is a must have if you are into music, please check this one out.

I used to have a Bose Speaker, and I used to think that nothing would compare to the sound of the Bose. Well, this unit really and gladly surprised me. The sound quality and appearance of this is fantastic. The price is more than fair, simple and compact and it was very easy to pair to any device I had at home, lets say, tablet, ipad, iphone, android. Amazing deep bass and clear high range. Good idea that it charges with a standard wire. Good looking, kind of heavy for the small size, however it feels reliable and sturdy.

The battery last a lot, according to the specs it states that 30 hours at half volume, I haven’t had the chance to deplete it completely, but I have played music for 14 hours and it is still up and running. 

So if you are looking for a portable Bluetooth speaker that delivers good sound at an affordable price, this is the unit you are looking for, highly recommended!

======= Summary =======

The Audio Pro Addon T3 bluetooth speaker is a well-built, well-balanced, and high-quality audio device with a great design.


======= Design ======= 

Design and aesthetics are subjective but I think the design of the Audio Pro Addon T3 is fantastic~!! The rectangular white cabinet body with its round blacker speaker cutouts look dashing, stylish, modern, and elegant. The genuine leather black handle further enhances its design and is a nice touch. Touch is an important experience too and it just feels nice grabbing the speaker by its thick, quality leather strap handle instead of touching some cheap plastic piece.

The cabinet is also smooth to the touch and feels premium. So is the metal cut-out that houses all the metal buttons. Pressing the buttons gives great tactile feedback and they are a pleasure to use. No wiggling, no clickty annoying sounds. Just reassuring and firm button presses. 

The speaker weighs about 6 to 7 pounds and feels solid and dense in a quality sort of way. 

To sum it all up, the whole speaker unit is made of great build materials and the fit-and-finish is impeccable. It is pleasure to look at and a pleasure to handle. 


======= Connectivity Options =======

I appreciate that the Audio Pro T3 offers both a Bluetooth and AUX cable connection. My Samsung Galaxy S8+ quickly found and easily paired up with the Audio Pro in a seamless manner. I also tested out the speaker by connecting my desktop soundcard to the AUX port on the back of the speaker with a 3.5mm audio cable and everything worked great. 

Also present on the back of the speaker is a USB charging port for devices such as cellphones and tablets as well as a charging port for the speaker. The USB charging port only outputs 1A so its usefulness is kind of limited but it is still a nice option to have nonetheless. It is better to have than not have. 


======= Sound Quality ======= 

Sound quality is great~! From its two 2 & 3/4" 5W textile dome tweeters & its 15W 3.5" subwoofer (with a frequency range between 60 - 20,000 Mhz), music and audio flow out loudly, cleanly, and with good dynamic range and projection. 

The Audio Pro T3 handles all types of music very well - pop, hip-hop, rock, vocals, electronica, instrumental, etc. 

Turn up the volume and the speaker can really rumble and spit out tight and punchy bass. Vocal separation is excellent as are details in the strings.

The volume can get very loud and it can easily fill out small to medium-sized rooms. I was impressed by how far the audio was projected.

With the sound quality so good and clear, the Audio Pro T3 can even replace my mid-level 5.1 PC speaker system which is currently connected to my desktop gaming PC.


======= Is it any good? Is is a good buy? =======

Most definitely~! I will recommend the Audio Pro Addon T3 speaker without any hesitation to anyone who is looking for a high-quality, well-built, good-looking portable speaker with excellent sound quality. I wasn't familiar with the Audio Pro brand prior to using this speaker but they have created a very positive impression on me.

This is a very substantial speaker, both in style and in weight. The heft of the speaker likely comes from a huge battery, as the device uses a household 120V cord to charge itself, rather than a common USB cord that lighter speakers use.

Pros
====
* Gorgeous design. This speaker will look good in every interior design style, except for perhaps something Victorian or rustic.
* Bluetooth pairing with an iPad and Android phone was almost instantaneous.
* Very satisfying sound. Three front-firing speakers for high and mid-range and a passive subwoofer in the back should accommodate most musical tastes.
* No distortion at full volume listening to both Olivia Newton-John and Pantera, two very different artists that put different demands on a speaker.
* Very strong bass, if that's your taste.

Cons
====
* Very strong bass, with no equalizer on the device itself. 
* Very hefty; once it's set down on a desk, don't expect to be casually moving it around.


This speaker is plenty capable of being portable, but I don't see myself doing that a whole lot. With its claimed 30 hours of portable use at 50% volume, it'd be perfect for putting wherever you want. That half-volume is still plenty loud, but you always have to return it to its standard power cord tether, which you likely left dangling out of the wall when you last charged it.
